title: Backfill tooling for 2025Q1 unit economics aggregates (idempotent + throttled)
preview: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while prot...
text: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while protecting the warehouse and avoiding double-counting. Scope: (1) Adds a backfill CLI (unit-econ-backfill) that replays the same aggregation logic as the hourly job over a time range; (2) Implements idempotency via per-hour partition overwrite semantics and a backfill_runs audit table; (3) Adds throttling (max concurrent partitions, adaptive sleep on warehouse pressure) and a maintenance-window mode; (4) Adds verification queries and a reconciliation helper view to compare pre/post backfill totals; (5) Updates runbook docs referenced by alerts. Idempotency model: the backfill o...

title: Fix throughput metric double-counting for streaming responses
preview: Problem: Dedicated throughput SLO reporting was overcounting tokens for streaming requests. We were incrementing `deployment_tokens_generated_total` per-stream chunk (delta tokens) and then incrementing again on stream completion using the final `usage.completion_tokens` value. For non-streaming, the completion-path...
text:
Problem: Dedicated throughput SLO reporting was overcounting tokens for streaming requests. We were incrementing `deployment_tokens_generated_total` per-stream chunk (delta tokens) and then incrementing again on stream completion using the final `usage.completion_tokens` value. For non-streaming, the completion-path increment is correct; for streaming, it results in ~2x token totals (shape/model dependent) and skews derived tokens/sec in the SLO evaluator + dashboards.

Customer/ops impact: This primarily affects Dedicated throughput SLO compliance tracking (tokens/sec floor) and internal saturation indicators. It also contributed to customer confusion when comparing Console throughput graphs vs request-level `usage` totals (see SUP-18510). Billing exports are not impacted (billing uses metering events, not the SLO throughput counters).

Fix: Make streaming accounting single-source-of...

title: Add tests for customer tier mapping edge cases (dedicated/private flags, pilot credits)
preview: Context: We’ve had a few near-misses (and one minor backfill discrepancy) where `customer_tier` was derived differently depending on which upstream flags were present (dedicated routing metadata vs contract flags) and where some pilot/credit-only customers were incorrectly classified as enterprise. This PR adds targ...
text: Context: We’ve had a few near-misses (and one minor backfill discrepancy) where `customer_tier` was derived differently depending on which upstream flags were present (dedicated routing metadata vs contract flags) and where some pilot/credit-only customers were incorrectly classified as enterprise. This PR adds targeted test coverage around tier derivation to prevent regressions as we continue to iterate on mapping rules for the Unit Economics dashboard. Changes: (1) Adds a comprehensive unit test matrix for tier derivation covering precedence rules between `dedicated`, `private`, `enterprise_contract`, `reseller`, and `pilot_credits`/`free_credits` flags. (2) Adds “golden” fixtures for known edge cases pulled from real anonymized records (IDs removed; synthetic account keys used). (3) Adds a few property-style tests to assert invariants (e.g., dedicated/private always win over self-s...

title: Bugfix: preserve rows when region mapping is null/unknown in unit economics aggregation
preview: Context: After merging the region normalization work, we noticed intermittent drops in hourly unit economics aggregates where a small (but non-trivial) slice of metering rows had region unset or had a value that didn’t map (e.g. legacy colo labels, nulls from older gateway paths, or "unknown" emitted during partial ...
text:
Context: After merging the region normalization work, we noticed intermittent drops in hourly unit economics aggregates where a small (but non-trivial) slice of metering rows had region unset or had a value that didn’t map (e.g. legacy colo labels, nulls from older gateway paths, or "unknown" emitted during partial outages). The aggregation job used an INNER JOIN to the canonical region mapping table, which caused those rows to be dropped entirely. This under-counted output_tokens and made $/1M output tokens look artificially worse/better depending on which spend inputs were present for that hour.

What this PR does: Update the unit economics aggregation query and the region mapping helper to be null-safe and non-dropping: (1) switch the join to LEFT JOIN, (2) COALESCE to a canonical region value of "unknown" when mapping fails, and (3) emit an explicit counter for unmapped rows so we...

title: Backpressure controls + max lag safety (auto-pause) for downstream outages
preview: Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit bac...
text:
Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit backpressure controls and a max-lag safety mechanism to keep the system stable during downstream outages while preserving at-least-once semantics.

What’s in this PR: (1) Max lag threshold per destination with auto-pause: if export_lag_seconds for a (tenant, destination) exceeds configurable max_lag_seconds for a sustained window, we stop pulling new batches for that destination and mark it PAUSED (reason=MAX_LAG) while continuing to process other destinations/tenants. (2) Backpressure based on in-flight bytes and retry backlog: introduce per-destination in_flight_bytes limits...

title: Audit event schema v2: add event_id + category taxonomy (compat mapping)
preview: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta (ENG-5127), downstream delivery is at-least-once and we need stable dedup keys plus a consistent category taxonomy for filtering + mapping into Splunk/Datadog. This PR updates core audit event emission to include: (1) `event_id` (stable unique id per event) (2) `schem...
text:
Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta (ENG-5127), downstream delivery is at-least-once and we need stable dedup keys plus a consistent category taxonomy for filtering + mapping into Splunk/Datadog. This PR updates core audit event emission to include: (1) `event_id` (stable unique id per event) (2) `schema_version: 2` and canonical `category` / `subcategory` / `action` fields (3) a compatibility mapping layer so existing producers that still emit legacy `event_type`/`operation` continue to work while we progressively migrate call sites.

Summary of changes: Add `event_id` generated at emission time using UUIDv7 (time-ordered, collision-safe) and plumbed through the AuditEvent struct. Introduce an enum-backed category taxonomy with a small canonical set and a `category_version` constant to make future taxonomy evolution explicit. Add a `legacy_event_type` field (optional) an...

title: Bootstrap audit exporter service skeleton + CI + local dev harness
preview:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; ...
text:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; those will follow in subsequent PRs.

## Motivation / Context
We need a dedicated service to read audit events (initially via stubbed source in local dev), apply tenant scoping, and deliver to downstream destinations with retries/checkpointing. Before we can iterate on architecture and adapters, we need:
- Standard project layout + build tooling
- Baseline CI (lint/test/build, container build)
- A config model (typed) that can evolve with control-plane config sync
- A local harness so devs can run the service end-to-end without needing the full control plane

## What’s incl...

title: Add config sync from control plane (polling + streaming) with tenant scoping + caching
preview: Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memo...
text:
Implements config sync from the control plane for the audit exporter service. This adds (1) tenant scoping so a given exporter instance only processes configs it is authorized to see, (2) a resilient polling loop with jitter/backoff and a long-poll “watch” mode when supported by the control plane, and (3) an in-memory snapshot cache with safe defaults so we do not accidentally start exporting for a tenant unless the config is explicitly enabled.

Motivation
- ENG-5139 created the exporter scaffold; we need an end-to-end way to pull export destinations/filters from the control plane without requiring restarts.
- ENG-5132 is landing CRUD + validation in the control plane; this PR wires up the consumer side.

Key behaviors
- Startup: exporter boots in “no tenants enabled” state until it successfully fetches a config snapshot.
- Polling: periodic snapshot fetch (default 10s with +/-20% ji...

title: Add dashboards + alerts for data residency enforcement (blocked requests, policy eval latency)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be...
text: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be prevented once enforcement is enabled. Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement rollout (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocking), SRE and Security asked for a consistent set of metrics visualizations and actionable alerts. This pack is intended to be deployed alongside the routing/gateway changes and used during dogfood and progressive rollout. What’s included: Grafana dashboard: “Data Residency Enforcement” with panels: Overview: total requests, allowed/blocked, block-rate (%), and top block reasons (5m/1h). Blocked requests by region + endpoint...

title: Add per-deployment throughput metrics (tokens/sec) + metric semantics doc
preview: Context / Why: Dedicated tiering work needs a canonical, per-deployment throughput signal that SRE and Product can use for the throughput SLO. Today we only have aggregated tokens/sec at the cluster/pool level, which is not sufficient for customer-facing guarantees and makes triage hard when multiple deployments sha...
text:
Context / Why: Dedicated tiering work needs a canonical, per-deployment throughput signal that SRE and Product can use for the throughput SLO. Today we only have aggregated tokens/sec at the cluster/pool level, which is not sufficient for customer-facing guarantees and makes triage hard when multiple deployments share infrastructure. ENG-19244 tracks adding a canonical per-deployment metric with consistent semantics across streaming/non-streaming and across routes (chat/completions). This PR adds the first version of those metrics (counters + derived rates), standard tags/labels, and initial documentation describing how to interpret them.

What’s in this PR: (1) New counters emitted at request completion for prompt/completion tokens, attributed to deployment_id and model, with route + region + tier tags. (2) A derived throughput panel-friendly series (tokens/sec) implemented as record...

title: Add audit log events for residency policy create/update/delete + wire to audit-log-exporter
preview: Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement we need auditable change history for residency pinning configuration (org/project/api-key level). This PR emits audit log events on residency policy mutations (create/update/delete) and wires those events into the existing audit-log-exporter pipeline so they are ...
text: Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement we need auditable change history for residency pinning configuration (org/project/api-key level). This PR emits audit log events on residency policy mutations (create/update/delete) and wires those events into the existing audit-log-exporter pipeline so they are available in Console audit log views and customer exports. Scope: Control plane (residency policy service) emits events; exporter normalizes/ships; no changes to request-time enforcement here. Event types (v1): residency_policy.created, residency_policy.updated, residency_policy.deleted, residency_policy.override_applied (admin-only, gated behind flag). Payload highlights: actor (user_id/service, auth_type, ip where available), target (org_id, project_id nullable, api_key_id nullable), policy_id, allowed_regions, primary_region, allow_cross_region_fallback, effective_from, ...

title: Add Datadog Logs destination adapter + audit tag/facet mapping
preview: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preservi...
text: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preserving at-least-once semantics. Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we support Splunk HEC, S3, and Datadog Logs. Datadog differs from Splunk in that (a) intake is strongly rate-limited, and (b) the best user experience is via consistent tagging/faceting for filtering/dashboards in Datadog. This PR aligns with ENG-5156 and the delivery semantics ADR (at-least-once, downstream dedup via event_id). Design notes (high-level): Adapter: Adds `datadog_logs` destination type with `site`, `intake_path`, and `api_key_secret_ref`. We send batches to `https://http-intake....

title: Add Splunk HEC destination adapter (gzip compression, 429 backoff, per-token circuit breaker)
preview: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persiste...
text: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persistent throttling. Motivation: Beta customers are primarily Splunk-first and we observed exporter lag spikes when HEC returns sustained 429s; this change improves delivery stability and reduces noisy retry behavior. Scope: Adapter implementation + config schema + basic unit tests + metrics hooks. Not included: Splunk indexer acknowledgment (HEC ACK) support (will track separately), advanced event field remapping beyond the audit schema’s SIEM mapping guidance. Delivery semantics: remains at-least-once; downstream Splunk consumers should dedupe on event_id if needed. Config: add...

title: Add dead-letter queue + guarded replay CLI for failed export batches
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently d...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently drop events while still protecting the platform and customers from accidental replay storms. Context / Motivation: During downstream outages (e.g., sustained 429s from Splunk HEC or 403s from mis-scoped Datadog keys), the exporter currently retries with backoff and then surfaces a per-destination error. Without DLQ persistence we risk either (a) losing batches on process/queue churn or (b) having no safe operational path to replay specific failures once a customer fixes credentials/quotas. ENG-5164 calls for a DLQ + replay tooling with explicit operator controls. Design high...

title: Unit economics: add reconciliation report vs billing totals (daily + monthly)
preview: Adds a reconciliation report and warehouse view that compares the Unit Economics pipeline aggregates (blended $/1M output tokens and underlying $ + tokens) against billing totals for the same time window. This is intended as a Finance sign-off tool and a recurring data quality guardrail for exec reporting (QTD/MTD)....
text: Adds a reconciliation report and warehouse view that compares the Unit Economics pipeline aggregates (blended $/1M output tokens and underlying $ + tokens) against billing totals for the same time window. This is intended as a Finance sign-off tool and a recurring data quality guardrail for exec reporting (QTD/MTD). The report is internal-only and intentionally conservative about what it claims to reconcile: we reconcile compute-related cost attribution and token-metered usage to billing line items that are attributable to inference usage; we explicitly exclude taxes, one-time fees, manual adjustments, and contract-specific true-ups that are not represented in metering. Implementation overview: (1) introduces a canonical reconciliation window definition (UTC by default, with a documented option to run in Finance reporting TZ), (2) materializes daily and monthly reconciliation tables k...

title: Implement S3 destination adapter (batching, partitioning, checkpoints, retries)
preview:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
-...
text:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
- New S3 destination adapter implementing the exporter Destination interface
- Batching policy: flush on max_events, max_bytes, or flush_interval (defaults tuned for beta)
- Partitioning scheme (configurable prefix + time partitioning + tenant scoping) to avoid hot prefixes and keep search-friendly layouts
- Checkpoint persistence for at-least-once semantics (write object -> persist checkpoint). Cursor is (emitted_at, event_id) and is treated as an opaque ordering token by downstream
- Retry/backoff for S3 throttling/transient errors (SlowDown/503/timeouts) with jitter and a...

title: Add quantization_profile to request metadata + metering (with allowed-value enforcement)
preview:
Context

Unit Economics dashboard v1 needs a stable dimension for grouping cost/1M output tokens by quantization profile (fp16 vs int8 vs 4-bit, etc). We currently have model/version, region, tier, and streaming flag in metering; quantization profile is only implicitly encoded in some model ids and can’t be reliably...
text:
Context

Unit Economics dashboard v1 needs a stable dimension for grouping cost/1M output tokens by quantization profile (fp16 vs int8 vs 4-bit, etc). We currently have model/version, region, tier, and streaming flag in metering; quantization profile is only implicitly encoded in some model ids and can’t be reliably joined in analytics. This PR adds an explicit quantization_profile tag to request metadata and ensures it’s emitted to metering with cardinality guardrails.

What this PR does

- Adds quantization_profile to the request metadata object that flows from gateway -> runtime -> metering.
- Emits quantization_profile as a first-class metering dimension for token usage (input/output) and request-level latency metrics.
- Enforces allowed values at the gateway boundary to prevent high-cardinality or free-form strings from polluting metrics. Unknown/unset is normalized to "unknown"....

title: Derive customer_tier dimension for unit economics + backfill tooling
preview:
Context: Unit Economics dashboard needs a finance-aligned, stable `customer_tier` dimension to support drilldowns and reconciliation. Today tier is implicitly inferred ad-hoc in queries (billing plan here, dedicated tags there), which creates mismatches and makes backfills painful.

This PR introduces a canonical de...
text:
Context: Unit Economics dashboard needs a finance-aligned, stable `customer_tier` dimension to support drilldowns and reconciliation. Today tier is implicitly inferred ad-hoc in queries (billing plan here, dedicated tags there), which creates mismatches and makes backfills painful.

This PR introduces a canonical derivation path for `customer_tier` and a safe backfill tool to populate the dimension for historical metering events used by the aggregation job.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a canonical `customer_tier` derivation function used in the metering/analytics ingestion path. Output values are a controlled enum: `self_serve | dedicated | private | enterprise | unknown`. 2) Establishes the source-of-truth precedence order (documented in code + comments) so we get deterministic outcomes across services: (a) Explicit contract metadata (enterprise/private flags) from Billing/Contracts s...

title: Add hourly unit economics aggregation job + warehouse schema/materialized views
preview: Context: This PR implements the v1 hourly aggregation job for the internal Unit Economics dashboard. It introduces warehouse schema migrations, a scheduled aggregator (hourly) that joins token metering with cost inputs (GPU rate card + allocation factors), and initial materialized views to power cost/$ per 1M output...
text: Context: This PR implements the v1 hourly aggregation job for the internal Unit Economics dashboard. It introduces warehouse schema migrations, a scheduled aggregator (hourly) that joins token metering with cost inputs (GPU rate card + allocation factors), and initial materialized views to power cost/$ per 1M output tokens drilldowns by model, region, and customer tier. This is the first production path for the canonical metric (blended $/1M output tokens) and is intended to be finance-aligned while still allowing iteration via versioned logic. Scope: (1) New warehouse tables for hourly aggregates and reconciliation-friendly rollups, (2) A job that runs hourly with late-arrival handling and idempotent upserts, (3) Materialized views for dashboard queries (time series + top-N breakdowns), (4) Data validation checks to fail fast on missing dimensions and prevent silent drops. Non-goals:...

title: Fix streaming output token metering parity + add regression tests
preview: Context: We found a consistent under-count of output tokens for streaming chat/completions compared to the non-streaming path, which makes blended $/1M output tokens look artificially high for streaming-heavy workloads and breaks parity in the unit economics aggregation. Root cause: the streaming emitter was increme...
text: Context: We found a consistent under-count of output tokens for streaming chat/completions compared to the non-streaming path, which makes blended $/1M output tokens look artificially high for streaming-heavy workloads and breaks parity in the unit economics aggregation. Root cause: the streaming emitter was incrementing output_tokens from partial deltas, but skipped accounting for (a) tool/function-call argument tokens in streamed tool calls, and (b) the final “finish_reason” frame when the last delta was empty. In addition, the truncation path (max_tokens / server-side truncation) could exit early without emitting the final accounting event. Changes in this PR: (1) Move streaming output token accounting to a single authoritative counter that is updated from the same tokenization boundary used for billing/metering (token_count_v2) rather than from per-chunk heuristics. (2) Ensure we ...
